title: Race 3 Recap - Fromme XC
content:
Great job everyone on the race yesterday — there was some great riding out there!

Results are up and updated. Take a look at how you did.

Race Notes:
- A rider was injured on Bobsled after contact from a rider behind. The following rider did not stop, but thankfully a teammate stepped in to help get him and his bike safely off the trail. Huge shoutout to Daniel from Argyle for stepping up — that’s what this community is all about.
- When passing, please make sure it is done safely and clearly communicate with the rider ahead.
- Big effort from Dash (Pemberton) in the Bantam Boys division — after a long pre-ride, he started from behind and still rode his way into 10th place. Impressive ride!

Next Week: Fromme Enduro
Get out this weekend and pre-ride!

title: Race Update – Fromme Course Clarifications
content:
A few important course clarifications after pre-riding this week:

Start Order (from 3:30 pm):
Junior Boys
Senior Boys
Jr/Sr Girls
Juvenile Boys
Bantam Boys
Juv/Bant Girls

Riders will be sent in waves. Each group will start once the previous group has completed their Bobsled lap and cleared the start area. Please be ready to go when your division is called.

Finish Line:
The finish is partway up Roadside Attraction (not the top)
Look for a large flagged exit on the left about 2/3 of the way up
Timing stops as soon as riders make the turn back onto the road.

King of the Shore Exit:
A new exit has been built, but it will be closed/flagged off for this race
All riders must use the regular KOS exit

Drop Off & Parking:
No drop-off at the top of Fromme (limited space with registration and medics)
Use drop-off areas near the Baden Powell exit or the Container
Riders should not hang out in the parking lot to keep traffic flowing

It’s not a long race, but it should be a great one. See you Wednesday!

title: Seymour Enduro Results Are Live! 🚵‍♂️
content:
What a day out there! The Seymour Enduro results are now live and fully updated, including individual standings and overall team results.

Times are tight and there were some great rides across all divisions — make sure you check out how you stacked up!

Full stage times and results are available on Zone4:
View Zone4 Results

title: Race 2 - Seymour Enduro Recap
content:
Great job everyone! The rain held off, although I heard there was even some snow on course. It sounds like everyone had a great time and, most importantly, there were no major incidents.

Zone4 has posted the individual stage results, but there are still a few anomalies. If you raced but are showing a DNS, don’t worry — we will sort it out. That means some placements could still change, so please check back for updates.

A huge thank you to Handsworth coach Braden Holt for flagging the course, and a big thank you as well to all of our volunteers. We rely on volunteers to help run our enduros, and your support is greatly appreciated.

Next race: Fromme XC

View Seymour Enduro stage results on Zone4

title: Race 2: Course Change
content:
Race 2: Course Change!

After the pre-ride and current wet conditions, it has been decided that we will NOT be racing on Pangor. The updated course is as follows:

Grade 8/9
Rider briefing: 2:45 pm at Parkgate Park

Stages:
  • Stage 1: John Deer (from the bell)
  • Stage 2: Empress Bypass
Route:

Riders will climb up Old Buck → Bridal Path → Academy Climb → RT Connector → Penny Lane → Good Sir Martin.

After Stage 1 (John Deer), riders will return across Bridal Path and descend Empress Bypass, then return to Parkgate for registration.


Grade 10/11/12
Rider briefing: 3:00 pm (approx.)

Stages:
  • Stage 1: Pingu
  • Stage 2: Severed D
  • Stage 3: John Deer
Route:

Riders will climb Old Buck → Powerline to Stage 1.

After Stage 1, riders will travel across Baden Powell → Mushroom → Powerline to Stage 2.

Stage 2 finishes at GSM. Riders will then climb back up to start Stage 3 (John Deer).

After Stage 3, riders will return via Bridal Path, but will exit via the Perimeter Trail to avoid conflict with Grade 8/9 riders on Empress Bypass, before returning to Parkgate Park.

title: Race 1 Results Update
content: There has been an update to the Race 1 results for the Juvenile Boys division due to some initial confusion with categories. The results have now been reviewed and corrected, and will remain as posted.

title: Race 1 Complete!
content:
Race 1 is in the books — and what a great start to the season!

Great job to everyone on your first race of the season. It was awesome to see such a strong turnout and positive energy across all divisions — hope you all had a great time out there.

A big thank you to Mr. Kelsh (Windsor) for flagging the course, as well as all of our volunteers and MedVent First Responders for keeping everyone safe and helping out throughout the day.

Race results are now updated and available on the Results page.

Next up: Seymour Enduro
We are looking for volunteers — parents can sign up on the race page.

title: The 2026 Season Begins This Week!
content:
Race #1: Richard Juryn is this Wednesday, April 8. Please check the race page for race details, parking, and course information.

Coaches:
Roster updates can be sent to Michelle at mahoy@sd44.ca.
Race package pickup will be at registration.
We need more volunteers for the race — please share the website link with parents.

Volunteers Needed!
If you are able to volunteer for a race to help with marshaling, please check out the race pages for volunteer opportunities. Students can also volunteer for hours.

title: Trail Day with NSMBA – April 1
content: Great job to Argyle and Sutherland for coming out to support our trail network and work with the NSMBA despite the wet weather. Pictures coming soon!
